title: Lavender and oat drawer sachets
subtitle: Dried lavender and oat sachets for scenting drawers and wardrobes
excerpt: Small muslin sachets filled with dried lavender flowers, porridge oats, and a few drops of lavender essential oil. Tuck into drawers, hang in wardrobes, or place in linen cupboards. The lavender provides a calming scent and acts as a mild moth deterrent. Refresh with a few drops of essential oil when the scent fades.
sourceNotes: Dried lavender as a traditional moth deterrent and textile scenting agent: widely documented in household management literature. Tisserand and Young, Essential Oil Safety (2nd ed., 2014): lavender for general domestic fragrance applications.

recipe.yieldDescription: 6 to 8 sachets (depending on muslin sachet size)
recipe.batchNotes: Make a larger batch for all wardrobes and drawers at once. Store unused sachets sealed in a bag until needed.
recipe.makeAheadNotes: Make well ahead; the sachets improve as the lavender essential oil blends into the dried flowers over a few days.
recipe.shelfLifeNotes: Refresh with a few drops of lavender essential oil every 3 to 6 months when the scent fades. The sachets themselves are effective indefinitely if kept dry. Discard if any dampness enters the sachet, which can cause mould.

glossaryTerms.definition: Lavender essential oil contains linalool and linalyl acetate, compounds that moths (particularly the clothes moth, Tineola bisselliella) find repellent. Dried lavender sachets in wardrobes are a traditional alternative to chemical moth balls. The effect is deterrent, not lethal; live moths already present will not be killed. Replace or refresh sachets every few months and check stored woolens and silks regularly.

Porridge oats add bulk to the sachet and act as a carrier for the essential oil: the porous structure of the oat flakes absorbs the lavender EO and releases it slowly over time, extending the scent duration of the sachet beyond what dried flowers alone provide.

content.text: Drawer and wardrobe sachets scent stored textiles and act as a mild
. This blend combines dried lavender flowers for fragrance and deterrence with porridge
that extends the life of the lavender essential oil. The sachets can be refreshed with a few drops of essential oil when the scent fades rather than making new ones each time.

Lavender essential oil is toxic to cats; keep the sachets away from pets. The sachets must be kept dry; any moisture will cause the oats to go mouldy. Do not place sachets in drawers with very damp items or in unventilated damp spaces.

items.prepNote: Apothecary-grade dried Lavandula angustifolia flowers. The primary fragrance and deterrent ingredient.
Standard porridge oats. Adds bulk and absorbs the essential oil, extending the scent life of the sachet.
Mix into the oats first; the oats absorb and distribute the EO through the filling. Lavandula angustifolia. Toxic to cats.

content.text: In a bowl, add the lavender essential oil to the porridge oats a few drops at a time, stirring to coat. Allow to sit for 5 minutes so the oats absorb the oil. Add the dried lavender flowers and mix gently to combine.

Cut the muslin cloth into squares approximately 15 cm x 15 cm. Place 2 tablespoons of filling in the centre of each square. Gather the corners, twist, and secure tightly with a piece of ribbon or string. Alternatively, use pre-made muslin drawstring bags. Each sachet should be firm enough to hold its shape and not leak.

Place sachets in drawers, on wardrobe shelves, or hang from a wardrobe rail. Squeeze the sachet gently each time you open the drawer to release more scent. When the scent fades (typically after 3 to 6 months), open the sachet and add 5 to 6 drops of lavender essential oil to the filling, reseal, and replace.

Keep in a dry location only. The sachets can be refreshed with lavender essential oil every 3 to 6 months indefinitely, as long as they stay dry. Discard any sachet that shows signs of dampness, mould, or an off smell. Store any spare sachets in a sealed bag until needed.
